upcoming_phases.title: Vault and sync architecture
upcoming_phases.rationale: Vault snapshot metadata now carries stable vault and device identifiers, so the next product phase can focus on encrypted sync semantics, conflict resolution, and shared-vault policy instead of inventing the snapshot contract.

upcoming_phases.title: Live GitHub release workflow verification
upcoming_phases.rationale: The CI release workflow and GitHub publish script are implemented and dry-run validated, but they still need one live repository-backed execution with production secrets.

risk_remediation_phases.title: Multi-surface regression hardening
risk_remediation_phases.rationale: Browser mode and native mode still use different transport stacks, so long-lived regression coverage needs a more explicit cross-surface matrix than the current browser validate path plus native fixtures.

risk_remediation_phases.title: Local fixture sandbox hardening
risk_remediation_phases.rationale: The broader localhost SSH fixtures produce valid results when run unsandboxed, but desktop sandboxing still obscures them during local validation and should be hardened into a documented, lower-friction path.

risk_remediation_phases.title: Notarization and release promotion
risk_remediation_phases.rationale: The packaging phase now produces signed release artifacts and logs, but the macOS bundle still fails Gatekeeper assessment until notarization, stapling, and promotion are automated.

risk_remediation_phases.title: Release credential portability hardening
risk_remediation_phases.rationale: The release flow is now notarized and promoted locally, but it still depends on a machine-local `notarytool` keychain profile and has no CI-backed promotion credential path.

risk_remediation_phases.title: Live GitHub release workflow verification
risk_remediation_phases.rationale: The repository now has a CI release workflow and publish script, but the GitHub-hosted signing, notarization, promotion, and release-upload path still needs one live run with repository secrets.

tasks.title: Move native key inspection, generation, and known-host scanning into Tauri
Add native trust and key regression coverage plus repo validation updates
Update docs, roadmap, validation log, bootstrap, and shared state
Add packaging, signing, notarization, and release diagnostics hardening
Automate notarization, stapling, and release promotion for the macOS bundle
Expand regression coverage across browser and native transport surfaces
Move release credentials and promotion into a portable CI-backed release path
Reduce local desktop sandbox friction for macOS localhost SSH fixtures
Add stable vault metadata to local snapshot exports and imports
Run the GitHub-hosted macOS release workflow with live repository secrets
Normalize canonical roadmap, shared state, agents, and prompts for bootstrap execution
Install runtime guardrail validation and record bootstrap evidence
Enforce the runtime guardrail validator on pull requests

risks.title: Native trust and key tooling still proxied through the backend
risks.summary: Native mode previously proxied key inspection, key generation, and known-host scans through the Node backend.

risks.title: Browser and native transport paths diverge intentionally
risks.summary: Cross-surface runtime coverage now exercises both browser and native transport seams through targeted unit tests, the shared Vitest suite runs on macOS CI, and browser e2e plus native fixtures remain in the validation matrix.

risks.title: Local macOS SSH fixtures need an unsandboxed shell
risks.summary: Native trust and transport fixture scripts now run an explicit localhost sshd preflight before Cargo tests, so unsupported shells fail fast with deterministic guidance instead of partial SSH fixture errors.

risks.title: Native bundle still used a placeholder macOS bundle identifier
risks.summary: The signed bundle and release manifest still emitted `com.example.terminalworkspace`, which is invalid release metadata for a real macOS package.

risks.title: Signed macOS bundle is not notarized
risks.summary: The local release path now submits the signed archive to Apple, staples the accepted ticket, and produces a Gatekeeper-accepted app bundle plus a promoted stable manifest.

risks.title: Release automation still requires a machine-local notary profile
risks.summary: Release automation now supports App Store Connect key auth, Apple ID auth, and local profile fallback, and the repo includes CI-backed signing/notarization/publish workflow plus documented env contracts.

risks.title: GitHub release workflow has not been executed with live repository secrets
risks.summary: The CI release workflow and GitHub publish script are implemented and dry-run validated locally, but the repository has not yet executed that workflow with real signing and notarization secrets.

opportunities.title: Convert native parity into release-ready packaging
opportunities.summary: The native shell now produces signed bundles, versioned release archives, machine-readable manifests, and CI preview artifacts.

opportunities.title: Design encrypted vault and sync architecture
opportunities.summary: Local vault snapshots now carry stable `vaultId`, `sourceDeviceId`, and `snapshotId` metadata and imports adopt the incoming vault identity, giving the sync architecture a concrete metadata contract.

opportunities.title: Publish promoted notarized artifacts through CI-backed release channels
opportunities.summary: The repo now includes a CI release workflow plus a GitHub publish script and promoted release notes, and the remaining step is one live GitHub-backed verification run.

incidents.summary: The first validate pass failed because Playwright still expected pre-declutter host headings and the old Settings runtime copy.
Local macOS sshd fixtures failed inside the desktop sandbox; rerunning the native trust and transport suites unsandboxed produced valid passing results.
The signed packaging gate originally reported `spctl: not_accepted`, but the notarization phase resolved it by submitting the archive to Apple, stapling the ticket, and revalidating with Gatekeeper.
The first signed release rebuild surfaced stale forward/snippet test payloads and a reused Response test harness bug; both were fixed before the final validation pass.

decisions.title: Move native trust and key tooling into Tauri
decisions.summary: Native mode now handles key inspection, key generation, and known-host scanning in Rust via Tauri commands and system OpenSSH tooling, while browser mode keeps the backend path.

decisions.title: Split native validation by scope
decisions.summary: Keep `native:key` as the fast key regression, `native:trust` as the phase gate, and `native:fixtures` as the broader localhost transport suite that requires explicit unsandboxed execution.

decisions.title: Make macOS packaging explicit and machine-readable
decisions.summary: Use an explicit `MACOS_SIGN_MODE=require` path for local signed bundles, a `skip` preview path for CI artifact generation, and emit release manifests plus verification logs into `artifacts/release/`.

decisions.title: Automate local notarization and stable promotion
decisions.summary: Drive notarization with a local `notarytool` keychain profile, staple and validate the accepted app bundle, then promote the notarized artifact into a stable-channel directory with manifest and checksum outputs.

decisions.title: Prefer portable notary auth and CI-backed release publishing
decisions.summary: Release automation now resolves App Store Connect key auth first, Apple ID second, and local keychain profiles last, then promotes notarized artifacts into a GitHub-release-ready asset set and CI workflow.

decisions.title: Preflight localhost fixture capability before Cargo SSH suites
decisions.summary: The native trust and transport scripts now validate temporary localhost sshd startup and host scanning before running the Cargo fixtures so local failures are explicit and reproducible.

decisions.title: Carry stable vault metadata in local snapshot exports
decisions.summary: The exported local snapshot format now includes a shared vault identifier, source device identifier, and snapshot identifier so future sync work can build on stable identities instead of a flat config dump.
